scenarios.query:
Audit this build/config scan:
```text
.github/workflows/deploy.yml: uses: docker/build-push-action@v2 (tag, not SHA);
  deploys on push to main; test job exists but `continue-on-error: true`
Dockerfile: FROM node:latest; COPY .npmrc (contains registry auth token)
config/index.js: module.exports = { db: process.env.DB_URL || 'postgres://localhost' }
  -- no validation; prod fell back to localhost last month (incident #77)
```
expected_behavior: Flags the auth token copied into the image (.npmrc) as a baked-in secret to inject at runtime and rotate
Flags the unpinned base image and action tag, and the test job that can't block deploys (continue-on-error on a merge/deploy gate)
Flags the unvalidated config with a dangerous localhost fallback (fail fast at startup instead), citing the incident

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan:
```text
flags.yml:
  use_new_search: false   (owner none; added 2 years ago; referenced in 0 code paths)
  beta_invoices: true     (owner none; both branches still maintained, 9 months)
build.sh: curl https://internal.example/toolchain.sh | bash   (no checksum)
Makefile: build depends on developer-local /opt/sdk-11.0.2
```
expected_behavior: Flags the dead flag (referenced nowhere) for deletion and the long-lived ownerless flag for an owner + removal plan
Flags the curl-pipe-bash toolchain fetch with no pin/checksum as unreproducible and a supply-chain risk
Flags the dependence on machine-local state (/opt/sdk) breaking build reproducibility

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan:
```text
ci.yml: actions pinned by SHA; lint, types, tests, pip-audit all required to merge;
  python toolchain pinned via uv.lock; image by digest; build-once-promote
settings.py: pydantic BaseSettings validates every var at startup; defaults safe;
  secret scanner clean; each var documented
flags: one flag, owner @growth, removal ticket linked
```
expected_behavior: Reports no findings (config and build hygiene are sound)
Recognizes the pinned reproducible build, required gates, fail-fast validated config, and owned flag
Does not demand additional infrastructure beyond the project's needs
